task: Write a Python function median(nums) returning the middle value of the sorted list when the length is odd, or the average of the two middle values as a float when the length is even. An empty list returns None. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The code correctly handles empty lists, but could be improved with more detailed documentation.
trace: I first sorted the list to ensure consistency in the calculation. The length of the sorted list was used to determine whether it's odd or even. If the length is odd, the middle value was returned as a float since Python lists don't support integer division with floating-point results. If the length is even, the average of the two middle values was calculated and returned as a float. To handle an empty list, I checked for it at the beginning and returned None if necessary.
